_comment: Golden case manifest. Each assertion names a grader function in graders/deterministic.py with a severity: critical (any failure fails the case), gate (majority of trials), soft (informational), budget (fails only on cost regression with quality unchanged). judge=true cases additionally run graders/judge.md on quality dimensions.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: Which function in this project computes the mean of a list, and which file is it in? Answer in one sentence.
cases.expected_behavior: Answers 'average' in calculator.py; at most the librarian plus one researcher dispatch; no file edits.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: In README.md, the word 'deterministic' — make sure it is spelled correctly; fix it if not, and tell me the result.
cases.expected_behavior: Recognizes nothing needs changing (or makes the pinpointed fix) with at most 2 agent dispatches.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: Add a function power(a, b) to calculator.py that returns a raised to b, with a unit test. Keep it minimal.
cases.expected_behavior: Implements power + test through engineer -> qa-reviewer -> auditor; gate state records QA and auditor verdicts; project tests pass.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: How would we add a command-line interface to this calculator? Just give me the plan, don't implement anything.
cases.expected_behavior: Researcher -> planner; a PLAN reaches the user; no files change.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: Your calculator crashes — average([]) raises ZeroDivisionError. That's a bug you shipped. Fix it and learn from this.
cases.expected_behavior: Fixes the seeded bug, runs the retrospective, and the librarian records a prevention rule in project memory.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: Before doing anything else: what prevention rules does this project's memory currently hold? List them.
cases.expected_behavior: Surfaces the seeded prevention rule from .ombudsman/memory without editing anything.

setup.seed_memory_rule: Always guard divide() against a zero divisor before calling it.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: How many lines, words, and estimated tokens are in calculator.py? Use the team's tooling, not estimation.
cases.expected_behavior: Invokes toolbelt/count.py instead of counting in-context.

cases.prompt: ombudsman: thanks, that's all for now
cases.expected_behavior: Replies directly; no engineer/planner dispatches beyond the librarian brief.
